title: Increase structural variation within a session
overview: Extend the existing evolution system to drive structural shader uniforms (noise frequency, radial scale, twist amplitude, field spread) over time via seeded harmonic curves, so the point cloud, particle field, and wave field continuously morph their geometry — not just their colour and cadence. All changes happen through new uniforms passed to the GPU vertex shader; no per-frame CPU buffer regeneration is needed.

files_to_modify.description: Add structural evolution curves: noiseFrequency (controls spatial noise scale in vertex shader), radialScale (shell radius multiplier), twistStrength (Y-axis twist amplitude), fieldSpread (overall point spread factor). Each gets 3 seeded harmonics with slow periods (60-240s) and bounded amplitudes. Export these as new fields alongside existing colour/cadence evolution.

Add four new fields to VisualParams interface: noiseFrequency (base 1.0, range 0.5-2.0), radialScale (base 1.0, range 0.7-1.4), twistStrength (base 1.0, range 0.3-2.0), fieldSpread (base 1.0, range 0.8-1.3). Set base values from session seed in mapSignalsToVisuals.

Add four new uniforms (uNoiseFrequency, uRadialScale, uTwistStrength, uFieldSpread). Apply uRadialScale to base position before deformation. Multiply layeredNoise frequency inputs by uNoiseFrequency. Scale twist angle by uTwistStrength. Apply uFieldSpread as a final position multiplier before breathing scale.

Declare the four new uniforms in the ShaderMaterial uniforms object. Update draw() to pipe the evolved noiseFrequency, radialScale, twistStrength, and fieldSpread from frame.params into the shader uniforms each frame.

Apply structural evolution to CPU-updated particles: modulate base position spread by fieldSpread, scale drift amplitude by radialScale, and vary jitter frequency by noiseFrequency in the draw() loop.
Apply structural evolution to wave lines: modulate wave frequency by noiseFrequency, scale wave amplitude by radialScale, and adjust vertical spread by fieldSpread in draw().
No changes needed — FrameState already carries VisualParams which will include the new fields.

files_to_create.description: Test suite for structural evolution: verifies new fields exist in evolved output, values stay within valid ranges, consecutive-frame deltas are smooth (no popping), different seeds produce different structures, and 120s scan shows no hard resets.

steps.title: Extend VisualParams with structural fields
steps.details: Add four new fields to the VisualParams interface: noiseFrequency (number), radialScale (number), twistStrength (number), fieldSpread (number). In mapSignalsToVisuals, compute seeded base values: use simpleHash(sessionSeed + ':struct') to derive base noiseFrequency in [0.7, 1.3], radialScale in [0.85, 1.15], twistStrength in [0.5, 1.5], fieldSpread in [0.9, 1.1]. These serve as the static per-session starting points that evolution will drift from.
steps.testing: Existing mappings tests should still pass. Manually verify new fields are present in mapSignalsToVisuals output.

steps.title: Add structural harmonic curves to evolution system
steps.details:
Extend CurveParams with four new harmonic arrays: noiseFrequency, radialScale, twistStrength, fieldSpread. In buildCurves(), generate 3 harmonics for each:
- noiseFrequency: amplitude 0.05-0.15 per harmonic, periods 60-180s. Total drift ±0.15-0.45 from base.
- radialScale: amplitude 0.03-0.08 per harmonic, periods 80-200s. Total drift ±0.09-0.24.
- twistStrength: amplitude 0.1-0.25 per harmonic, periods 45-120s. Total drift ±0.3-0.75.
- fieldSpread: amplitude 0.02-0.06 per harmonic, periods 100-240s. Total drift ±0.06-0.18.

In evolveParams(), evaluate each curve and add to base, then clamp: noiseFrequency to [0.3, 2.5], radialScale to [0.5, 1.6], twistStrength to [0.1, 2.5], fieldSpread to [0.7, 1.4]. Remove the pass-through comments for these fields.
steps.testing: Run existing evolution tests — they must still pass. The T-012-12 test that checks non-evolved params pass through will need updating since structureComplexity still passes through but the new fields are evolved.

steps.title: Update vertex shader with structural uniforms
steps.details:
Add four uniform declarations: uniform float uNoiseFrequency; uniform float uRadialScale; uniform float uTwistStrength; uniform float uFieldSpread;

Apply in main():
1. After `vec3 pos = position;`, apply radial scale: `pos *= uRadialScale;`
2. In the twist section, multiply twistAngle by uTwistStrength: `float twistAngle = uBassEnergy * sin(t * 0.0003 + aRandom.x * TAU) * 0.3 * ma * uTwistStrength;`
3. In layeredNoise calls, multiply the spatial coordinates by uNoiseFrequency: e.g. `layeredNoise(pos.x * uNoiseFrequency, pos.y * uNoiseFrequency, t * 0.0001)`
4. Before breathing scale, apply field spread: `pos *= uFieldSpread;`

This ensures all structural variation is GPU-driven with zero CPU buffer churn.
steps.testing: Visual inspection — build and load the page. The scene should look the same initially (all new uniforms default to 1.0).

steps.title: Wire uniforms through pointCloud system
steps.testing: Build succeeds, no shader compilation errors in console.

steps.title: Apply structural evolution to particleField and waveField
steps.details:
ParticleField draw(): multiply base position spread by fieldSpread (scale bx/by/bz), scale bassDrift amplitude by radialScale, vary the jitter sine frequency multipliers by noiseFrequency.

WaveField draw(): multiply wave.frequency by noiseFrequency when computing sine, scale totalAmplitude by radialScale, multiply the y-spread ((wave.y - 0.5) * 6) by fieldSpread.

These are lightweight multiplications in the existing per-frame loops — no new allocations.
steps.testing: Switch between modes and verify all three systems show structural variation over time.

steps.title: Write structural evolution tests
steps.details:
Create test suite 'US-033: Structural evolution' with these tests:

T-033-01: evolveParams returns all four structural fields (noiseFrequency, radialScale, twistStrength, fieldSpread) as finite numbers.

T-033-02: At elapsedMs=60000, at least one structural field differs from base by >0.05 (verifying actual structural change over time).

T-033-03: Smoothness — consecutive millisecond samples (t to t+1) have structural field deltas < 0.001 (no popping).

T-033-04: No hard reset — scanning 0 to 120000ms at 16ms steps, max frame-to-frame delta for each structural field is bounded (noiseFrequency < 0.01, radialScale < 0.005, twistStrength < 0.02, fieldSpread < 0.005).

T-033-05: Seed variance — different seeds produce noticeably different structural evolution at 60s.

T-033-06: Range clamping — structural fields stay within valid bounds across 0-300s scan.

T-033-07: Determinism — same seed + elapsed produces identical structural output.

T-033-08: Existing evolved params (paletteHue, cadence, paletteSaturation) are unaffected by the addition of structural curves.
steps.testing: Run: npx vitest run tests/visual/structuralEvolution.test.ts — all tests pass.

steps.title: Update existing tests for new VisualParams fields
steps.details: Update baseParams fixtures in all test files to include the four new VisualParams fields with default value 1.0. Update T-012-12 (evolution passthrough test) to no longer assert that the new structural fields pass through unchanged — they are now evolved. Ensure pointCloud tests include new uniforms in their shader material expectations. Update any VisualParams type assertions that enumerate all fields.
steps.testing: Run full test suite: npx vitest run — all tests pass.

steps.title: Build verification and definition of done
steps.details: Run npm run build to verify production build succeeds. Load in browser and verify: (1) no console errors, (2) scene renders and visibly morphs its structure over 60+ seconds, (3) mute toggle and info overlay still work, (4) no forbidden storage APIs used. Verify with different seeds that sessions look structurally different.
steps.testing: npm run build succeeds. Manual verification of all acceptance criteria.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.At least one seeded structural parameter: Steps 1-2 add four structural parameters (noiseFrequency, radialScale, twistStrength, fieldSpread) to the evolution system with seeded harmonic curves. Steps 3-5 wire them through to all three geometry systems.
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.The scene avoids hard resets; transition: Evolution uses layered sine harmonics (same proven approach as existing hue/cadence evolution) — continuous functions with no discontinuities. Test T-033-04 verifies bounded frame-to-frame deltas across a full 120s scan.
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Structural evolution remains smooth (no: Test T-033-03 verifies consecutive millisecond samples have sub-0.001 deltas. The harmonic approach guarantees C∞ smoothness. Clamping ranges are wide enough that values rarely hit bounds.
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Different sessions produce noticeably di: Base structural values are seeded from sessionSeed (step 1), and evolution curves are seeded independently (step 2). Test T-033-05 verifies seed variance produces measurably different structures.

risks.risk: Structural parameter ranges too wide causing visual glitches
risks.mitigation: Conservative amplitude ranges chosen (e.g., radialScale 0.5-1.6, not 0-3). Clamp bounds prevent extreme values. Easy to tighten ranges post-testing without architectural changes.

risks.risk: Reduced-motion users see too much structural change
risks.mitigation: All structural shader effects are already multiplied by uMotionAmplitude (0.2 for reduced-motion). The radialScale and fieldSpread multipliers affect base position, which is intentional — they change structure, not velocity. If needed, we can attenuate evolution amplitude by motionAmplitude in evolveParams.

risks.risk: Existing tests break due to new VisualParams fields
risks.mitigation: Step 7 explicitly handles updating all test fixtures. The new fields default to 1.0 (multiplicative identity) so existing visual behaviour is unchanged when evolution hasn't been applied.
